c74object.name: fluid.skmeans~
c74object.description: Uses K-means algorithm with cosine similarity to learn clusters and features from a DataSet.

discussion.p: fluid.skmeans~ is an implementation of KMeans based on cosine distances instead of euclidean ones, measuring the angles between the normalised vectors. One common application of spherical KMeans is to try and learn features directly from input data (via a fluid.dataset~) without supervision. See this reference for a more technical explanation: https://machinelearningcatalogue.com/algorithm/alg_spherical-k-means.html and https://www-cs.stanford.edu/~acoates/papers/coatesng_nntot2012.pdf for feature extractions.
Read more about fluid.skmeans~ on the learn platform.
fluid.skmeans~ is part of the Fluid Decomposition Toolkit of the FluCoMa project. For more explanations, learning material, and discussions on its musicianly uses, visit flucoma.org.

description.p: Name for this instance, so that it can be reused or referred to by other data objects. If none is given, a name will be generated

method.name: reset
method.digest: Return the object to its original state
method.description: Calling reset will change parameter values back to those the object was created with, i.e. any attribute values set in the box will be retained

method.name: refer
method.description: Changes the reference to the data in another named fluid.skmeans~ object. Changes to the data stored in any referenced will be shared by all other similarly named fluid.skmeans~ objects.

description.p: Identify numclusters clusters in a fluid.dataset~. It will optimise until no improvement is possible, or up to maxiter, whichever comes first. Subsequent calls will continue training from the stopping point with the same conditions.

ul.li:
dataSet:
A fluid.dataset~ of data points.

description.p: Given a trained object, return the cluster ID for each data point in a fluid.dataset~ to a fluid.labelset~.

ul.li:
dataSet:
A fluid.dataset~ containing the data to predict.

labelSet:
A fluid.labelset~ to retrieve the predicted clusters.

description.p: Given a trained object, return for each item of a provided fluid.dataset~ its encoded activations to each cluster as an array, often referred to as the cluster-activation space.

ul.li:
srcDataSet:
A fluid.dataset~ containing the data to encode.

dstDataSet:
A fluid.dataset~ to contain the new cluster-activation space.

description.p: Given a trained object, return the cluster ID for a data point in a buffer~

ul.li:
buffer:
A buffer~ containing a data point.

description.p: Given a trained object, return the encoded activation of the provided point to each cluster centroid. Both points are handled as buffer~

ul.li:
sourceBuffer:
A buffer~ containing a data point.

targetBuffer:
A buffer~ to write in the activation to all the cluster centroids.

description.p: Run fit and encode in a single pass: i.e. train the model on the incoming fluid.dataset~ and then return its encoded cluster-activation space in the destination fluid.dataset~

ul.li:
srcDataSet:
A fluid.dataset~ containing the data to fit and encode.

dstDataSet:
A fluid.dataset~ to contain the new cluster-activation space.

description.p: Given a trained object, retrieve the means (centroids) of each cluster as a fluid.dataset~

ul.li:
dataSet:
A fluid.dataset~ of clusters with a mean per column.

description.p: Overwrites the means (centroids) of each cluster, and declares the object trained.

ul.li:
dataSet:
A fluid.dataset~ of clusters with a mean per column.

description.p: Run fit and predict in a single pass: i.e. train the model on the incoming fluid.dataset~ and then return the learned clustering to the passed fluid.labelset~

ul.li:
dataSet:
A fluid.dataset~ containing the data to fit and predict.

labelSet:
A fluid.labelset~ to retrieve the predicted clusters.

description.p: The number of columns (dimensions) in this model or dataset / labeset
Reset the object status to not fitted and untrained.
The number of data points (entries / observations) in this model or dataset / labeset

method.name: load
method.description: Replace the internal state of the object from a dict. The message should take the form load dictionary <dictid>

method.name: dump
method.digest: Dump the state of this object from the dump outlet as a dict.

method.name: write
method.description: Save the internal state of the object to a JSON file on disk. With no argument, a file dialog will appear to choose the file. It is always executed at the lowest priority on the main thread, using deferlow internally.

method.name: read
method.description: Replace the internal state of the object from a JSON file on disk. With no argument, a file dialog will appear to choose the file. It is always executed at the lowest priority on the main thread, using deferlow internally.

description.p: The encoding threshold (aka the alpha parameter). When used for feature learning, this can be used to produce sparser output features by setting the least active output dimensions to 0.
The maximum number of iterations the algorithm will use whilst fitting.
The number of clusters to partition data into.
Enable warnings to be issued whenever a parameter value is constrained (e.g. clipped)
